skill.name: maxhub-wechat
skill.description: Query WeChat Channels and Official Account data through MaxHub APIs, including search, article details, comments, and account analysis.
skill.summary: Analyze WeChat Channels and Official Account data with MaxHub API references and workflows.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Static command and URL findings are mostly Markdown documentation or expected MaxHub API usage, and MAXHUB_API_KEY access is declared as a required credential. However, the references repeatedly provide an encrypted WeChat video decryption workflow using decode_key values and external tooling, which creates a high policy and abuse risk that needs human marketplace review.

high_findings.title: Encrypted WeChat Video Decryption Workflow
high_findings.description: The API reference repeatedly instructs users to combine media CDN URLs with tokens, extract decode_key values, and use third-party or self-hosted tools to decrypt encrypted WeChat video files. This is not a weak algorithm false positive; it is a semantic risk because it can facilitate bypassing platform media protections.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The decryption instructions are explicit and repeated across multiple endpoint sections. They include decode_key extraction, CDN token concatenation, and links to decryption tools.

medium_findings.title: Credential-Bearing Network Requests to Third-Party API
medium_findings.description: The skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY and sends it as an Authorization bearer token to https://www.aconfig.cn with curl examples. This is expected for the skill, but it creates secret-handling and third-party data exposure risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly declares MAXHUB_API_KEY as required and sensitive, and examples use it in Authorization headers. I found no evidence that the key is sent anywhere except the documented MaxHub API host.

medium_findings.title: Autonomous External HTTP Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The instructions tell the assistant to execute planned queries autonomously and include curl examples for external API calls. This is legitimate for an API skill, but it should be constrained to documented MaxHub endpoints and user-approved data requests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl commands and autonomous execution rules are present in the skill instructions. The risk is moderate because the destination is documented, not hidden or obfuscated.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are Markdown code spans, fenced install examples, API paths, JSON paths, and parameter names. These are documentation artifacts rather than executable code in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples are Markdown documentation and no script files exist in the scanned file list. The only executable pattern is the documented curl usage addressed separately.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Service References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to the MaxHub homepage, repository metadata, WeChat example links, and documented API endpoints. These are expected for a networked API reference skill, although they confirm network dependency.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and metadata references, not concealed callback endpoints. They still support the network risk factor because the skill depends on an external API.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The high-entropy and dangerous-combination heuristic appears to be caused by long bilingual Markdown API references containing URLs, curl examples, JSON paths, and credential placeholders. I found no evidence of binary payloads, encrypted hidden content, prompt injection, or concealed scripts in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanned package contains Markdown and JSON metadata only, with no executable script files in the file list. The risky combination is still represented by documented network and credential usage, which is covered in medium findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Encrypted WeChat Video Decryption Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The API reference repeatedly instructs users to combine media CDN URLs with tokens, extract decode_key values, and use third-party or self-hosted tools to decrypt encrypted WeChat video files. This is not a weak algorithm false positive; it is a semantic risk because it can facilitate bypassing platform media protections.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The decryption instructions are explicit and repeated across multiple endpoint sections. They include decode_key extraction, CDN token concatenation, and links to decryption tools.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Bearing Network Requests to Third-Party API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY and sends it as an Authorization bearer token to https://www.aconfig.cn with curl examples. This is expected for the skill, but it creates secret-handling and third-party data exposure risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly declares MAXHUB_API_KEY as required and sensitive, and examples use it in Authorization headers. I found no evidence that the key is sent anywhere except the documented MaxHub API host.

dangerous_patterns.title: Autonomous External HTTP Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions tell the assistant to execute planned queries autonomously and include curl examples for external API calls. This is legitimate for an API skill, but it should be constrained to documented MaxHub endpoints and user-approved data requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The curl commands and autonomous execution rules are present in the skill instructions. The risk is moderate because the destination is documented, not hidden or obfuscated.

content.value_statement: WeChat research often needs many endpoints and careful pagination. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through MaxHub queries and structured analysis.
actual_capabilities: Routes user requests between WeChat Channels and Official Account API references.
Explains required MaxHub API authentication with MAXHUB_API_KEY.
Supports search, detail, comment, article, account, and read count workflows.
Provides pagination and retry guidance for selected MaxHub endpoints.
Formats results in Chinese or English based on the user request.
Combines multiple endpoint results for comparison or trend analysis.
limitations: Requires a valid MaxHub API key and access to https://www.aconfig.cn.
Depends on third-party API availability, permissions, rate limits, and response quality.
Does not verify data accuracy beyond the API response.
Includes video decryption guidance that requires legal and policy review before use.

use_cases.title: Monitor Official Account Content
use_cases.description: Track article lists, details, comments, and related posts for competitor or owned WeChat accounts.

use_cases.title: Analyze WeChat Channels Performance
use_cases.description: Search videos, inspect details, review comments, and compare engagement signals across Channels content.

use_cases.title: Research Private Traffic Strategy
use_cases.description: Study accounts, articles, and audience interactions to summarize repeatable content patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Find WeChat Accounts
prompt_templates.prompt: Search WeChat Official Accounts for "人工智能" and summarize the most relevant accounts in Chinese.

prompt_templates.title: Review Recent Articles
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest articles for this Official Account ID and summarize themes, publishing cadence, and visible engagement data.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Channel Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Search WeChat Channels for "美食" and compare the top videos by topic, creator signals, and comment themes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Cross-Endpoint Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a structured report for this brand across Official Account articles and Channels videos, including search results, details, comments, and limitations.

output_examples.input: Search for WeChat Channels about 美食.
output_examples.output: A ranked summary of matching videos, creator names, visible engagement fields, and suggested follow-up queries.

output_examples.input: Analyze recent articles from a public account.
output_examples.output: A concise report covering article topics, publishing rhythm, comment signals, related articles, and missing data.

output_examples.input: Compare two WeChat accounts in English.
output_examples.output: A side-by-side comparison with audience positioning, content themes, activity patterns, and API data caveats.

best_practices: Use the narrowest endpoint that answers the request before starting multi-step analysis.
Keep MAXHUB_API_KEY out of prompts, logs, screenshots, and final responses.
Report API errors, empty results, and permission limits clearly in the final answer.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without a valid MaxHub API key and permission to query the data.
Do not treat third-party API results as verified facts without source context.
Do not use video decryption guidance to bypass platform controls or content rights.

faq.question: What data sources does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses MaxHub APIs for WeChat Channels and Official Account data.

faq.question: Does it require credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires MAXHUB_API_KEY for authenticated MaxHub API requests.

faq.question: Can it analyze both Chinese and English requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to match the user language.

faq.question: Can it fetch article comments?
faq.answer: Yes. The references include article comment and comment reply endpoints.

faq.question: Can it guarantee API data accuracy?
faq.answer: No. Results depend on the third-party API response and available permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish without review?
faq.answer: No. The encrypted video decryption workflow needs marketplace policy review.
